exercises.translation: I won't go to school today.

wrong_choice_explanations.ました: ました is the polite past form — it means 'did' but this sentence is about not doing something now.
wrong_choice_explanations.ないです: ないです is also polite negative, but ません is the standard form used with masu-stem verbs.
wrong_choice_explanations.ませんでした: ませんでした is polite past negative — it means 'didn't do' but this sentence is about present/future.

wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is the polite positive form — it means 'do/will do' but this sentence is negative.
wrong_choice_explanations.ました: ました is the polite past positive form — it means 'did' but this sentence is about not doing something.
wrong_choice_explanations.ないです: ないです is polite negative, but it's used with plain negative forms, not masu-stem verbs.

wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is the polite positive form — it means 'will watch' but this sentence expresses not wanting to watch.
wrong_choice_explanations.ませんでした: ませんでした is polite past negative — it means 'didn't watch' but this sentence is about future intention.
wrong_choice_explanations.たくない: たくない is used with the tai-form to express not wanting to do something, but the sentence structure requires ません.

wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is the polite positive form — it means 'eat' but this sentence is about not eating meat.
wrong_choice_explanations.ました: ました is the polite past positive form — it means 'ate' but this sentence is about general habit, not past action.
wrong_choice_explanations.なかった: なかった is plain past negative — it's too casual for this polite sentence structure.

wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is the polite positive form — it means 'will work' but this sentence is about not working.
wrong_choice_explanations.ませんでした: ませんでした is polite past negative — it means 'didn't work' but this sentence is about tomorrow.
wrong_choice_explanations.ない: ない is plain negative — it's too casual for this polite sentence structure.

wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is the polite positive form — it means 'will cook' but this sentence is about not cooking.
wrong_choice_explanations.ました: ました is the polite past positive form — it means 'cooked' but this sentence is about tonight (future).
wrong_choice_explanations.ないです: ないです is polite negative, but it follows plain negative forms like しない, not masu-stems.

exercises.translation: I won't participate in the party.

wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is the polite positive form — it means 'will participate' but this sentence is negative.
wrong_choice_explanations.ませんでした: ませんでした is polite past negative — it means 'didn't participate' but this sentence is about future decision.
wrong_choice_explanations.ないでしょう: ないでしょう expresses uncertainty about something being negative, but this sentence is a definite statement.

wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is the polite positive form — it means 'will read' but this sentence is about not reading.
wrong_choice_explanations.ました: ました is the polite past positive form — it means 'read' but this sentence is about future intention.
wrong_choice_explanations.なくて: なくて is a connective form meaning 'not doing and...' — it doesn't complete the sentence properly.

wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is the polite positive form — it means 'will go out' but this sentence is about staying home.
wrong_choice_explanations.ませんでした: ませんでした is polite past negative — it means 'didn't go out' but this sentence is about today's plan.
wrong_choice_explanations.ないつもり: ないつもり means 'intention not to do' but requires different sentence structure (するつもりはありません).

wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is the polite positive form — it means 'watch' but this sentence is about not watching TV.
wrong_choice_explanations.ました: ました is the polite past positive form — it means 'watched' but this sentence is about general habit.
wrong_choice_explanations.ないで: ないで means 'without doing' and is used to connect actions — it doesn't complete this sentence.
